extra.reasoning:
This is a state machine. Initial state: opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0. The property requires globally(opted_out implies not sent): no reachable state may have opted_out=true AND sent=true.

The flaw is the t_resume_glitch transition: on send_attempt, if (opted_out and ticks>=1), it sets sent:=true. This directly produces an opted_out=true, sent=true state, contradicting t_send_blocked (which would set sent:=false on the same event). Because ticks can reach 1, this glitch becomes reachable.

Counterexample trace (3 events, within the 5-event bound):
1. tick: guard true; ticks=0<1 so ticks:=1; sent:=false. State: opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=1.
2. optout: guard true; opted_out:=true, sent:=false. State: opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=1.
3. send_attempt: with opted_out=true and ticks>=1, t_resume_glitch is enabled and sets sent:=true. State: opted_out=true, sent=true, ticks=1.

After event 3 the system is in a state with opted_out=true and sent=true, so opted_out implies not sent is false. The property is violated. (Even under the competing t_send_blocked transition for the same send_attempt event, t_resume_glitch is enabled and can fire, so a violating execution exists.) The events file path is /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs/_judge_prompts/outreach-d1-viol-02.txt.
